header.primary_setup: Demand-Driven EPSU/EPT — DC segment displacing legacy FTTH via hyperscaler infrastructure + services pivot
header.primary_setup_verdict: confirmed present; DC +37% organic 2025, +62% Q4, margin 15.3%, asset-light model verified
header.secondary_setup: Corporate Change EPSU/EPT — new CEO Fröberg + decentralised M&A engine + SEK 120m cost programme
header.conviction: C — strengthening; DC growth confirmed, moat question partially answered, M&A execution remains the constraint
header.time_horizon: N1-3Y, fulcrum event inside 12m (BEAD resolution + DC growth re-affirmation in Q1-Q2 2026)

subsections.title: Financial forecasts -- VF modal case -- Watson analysis/judgement

B.title: EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
B.inline_verdict: Compelling mix-shift trade with genuine asymmetry; DC moat YELLOW, M&A ORANGE — ESA clears for starter position

blocks.text: ESA confirms the Triaging thesis with higher conviction. Hexatronic's DC segment is real, scaling, and profitable (Q4-25: +62% organic, 15.3% EBITA, asset-light). The hyperscaler AI buildout is a multi-year structural tailwind confirmed by Corning's $6bn Meta contract. HTRO's mid-market niche (Tier-2 cloud, regional DCs, enterprise) is less competed than Tier-1 hyperscaler direct supply. ESA-depth research confirms: (i) DC moat is narrow but defensible via local service execution + installed base stickiness — YELLOW, not ORANGE; (ii) M&A execution remains the binding constraint — Weterings write-off is a flag, but Fröberg's Ahlsell track record (20+ deals) is relevant. Overall IC strengthens from C+ to C+ (upper end). Starter position (1.0-1.5%) is justified at current entry (~SEK 28). Do NOT size up before Q1-26 DC print confirms.

ESA-depth analysis adds three layers beyond Triaging: (1) Sector primer confirms fibre optic industry is bifurcating between vertically integrated giants (Corning, Prysmian — preform + cable) and service-led integrators (HTRO niche). Preform manufacturers capture 70% of industry profit pool — HTRO cannot access this. But HTRO's 15%+ DC services margin is competitive with system integrator peers (~11.5% average). (2) Value chain analysis shows HTRO is deliberately moving downstream (design → install → day-two services) where switching costs are higher and competition from giants is less intense. The Communication Zone acquisition exemplifies this — local service capability in US Midwest for hyperscale clients. (3) Earnings history reveals a pattern: management under-promises after misses (H1-25) then over-delivers (H2-25). Q4-25 EBITA beat +10.8% is consistent with conservative guidance reset. This pattern is favourable for EPS revision momentum — expect continued positive surprises if DC sustains.

items.text: PRIMARY driver: DC segment scaling — fulcrum. Q4-25 organic growth +62% confirms momentum; 15.3% EBITA margin is 2x group average.

sub.text: DC + HE = ~40% of sales but ~60% of adj. EBITA by Q4-25. Mix shift is the numbers story.
Cloud/hyperscaler = 40% of DC sales; enterprise = 60%. Dual customer base reduces single-customer risk.
Management targets 3-6 DC acquisitions/year at 5-7x EBITDA, EV <EUR 50m. Pipeline appears real given US market fragmentation.

items.text: SECONDARY driver: BEAD programme materialisation — triggers legacy FTTH volume recovery in H2-26.

sub.text: $42.5bn US subsidy; 50 of 56 state proposals approved. 74% of early allocations still going to fibre despite technology-neutral rule change.
BEAD impact timing has been pushed right repeatedly. Management now guides H2-26; sell-side expects 2027 for meaningful revenue contribution.

items.text: TERTIARY driver: Harsh Environment defence/offshore tailwind — structural, secular, non-cyclical ballast.

sub.text: HE segment: +11% organic 2025, 11.2% EBITA margin rising toward 15% target. JOWO acquisition (EUR 11.8m, 4.7x EBITDA) adds European defence exposure.
NATO spending commitments provide 5-10 year visibility. Rochester Cable operational improvements are margin-accretive.

blocks.text: Key risk: the DC boom-bust parallel. Sell-side (Redeye) explicitly warns that hyperscaler capex could exhibit the same boom-bust as FTTH. The FTTH cycle saw Hexatronic's revenue surge 33% CAGR then collapse — if DC follows the same pattern, the 2028 targets are unachievable. Mitigant: DC business is services-led (design, installation, day-two) vs. FTTH which was product-distribution; services have higher switching costs and recurring revenue characteristics. This distinction needs pressure-testing at ESA.

C.title: REQUIRED ATTRIBUTES
C.inline_verdict: C+ (upper) — DC growth confirmed, moat narrow but defensible, M&A is the binding constraint

blocks.title: Ratings — all six pillars, Required Attribute Families, TCs, Required Attributes and Core Questions (HTRO, ESA)

rows.label: P1: Technical Strength
rows.summary: Post-trough recovery; MAs turning up but Stage 2 not confirmed. SP ~SEK 28, well below SEK 65+ peak.

rows.label: Minervini dimensions (8 point + extension)
rows.summary: Estimated 4-5 / 9 PASS — post-trough recovery, not confirmed Stage 2.

rows.label: D1: Price > 200D MA
rows.summary: SP ~SEK 28 above 200D MA ~SEK 22-23 (post-trough recovery).

rows.label: D2: 200D MA trending up (≥1M)
rows.summary: 200D MA has been declining; only recently flattening. Not confirmed uptrend.

rows.label: D3: Price > 150D MA
rows.summary: SP above 150D MA which is turning up.

rows.label: D4: 150D > 200D MA
rows.summary: Golden cross not yet confirmed — 150D still below 200D MA.

rows.label: D5: 50D > 150D MA
rows.summary: 50D MA has crossed above 150D on post-Q4 momentum.

rows.label: D6: Price > 50D MA
rows.summary: SP trading above 50D MA post-Q4 beat.

rows.label: D7: Price > 30% above 52W low
rows.summary: 52W low SEK 16.44; SP SEK 28 = +70% above low.

rows.label: D8: Price within 25% of 52W high
rows.summary: 52W high SEK 34.94; SP SEK 28 = -20% below high.

rows.label: D9: RS Rank ≥70 (provisional)
rows.summary: RS Rank not confirmed — need FactSet data. Estimated below 70.

rows.label: P2: Fit for Market Paradigm
rows.summary: AI/DC infrastructure paradigm-aligned; defence/HE fits risk-off. FTTH legacy is paradigm-negative.

rows.label: Paradigm 1: Late bull / early bear market
rows.summary: Mixed — DC/HE are defensive growth; FTTH is cyclically exposed. Net neutral.

rows.label: Paradigm 2: AI demand / disruption
rows.summary: Directly paradigm-aligned — DC fibre infrastructure is the physical layer of AI buildout.

rows.label: Paradigm 3: Oil, stagflation, recession, rising rates
rows.summary: No Iran exposure. HE defence/offshore benefits from geopolitical tensions. Tariff risk limited (<5% sales).

rows.label: P3: Fundamental Case (Outputs)
rows.summary: ESA confirms change forces and early outputs. DC organic +37% FY25, +62% Q4 with 15.3% EBITA margin. Moat narrow but defensible.

rows.label: IC#1: Required Case OUTPUTS
rows.summary: EPS path plausibly present but based on DC scaling + mix shift assumptions. Triple ratchet mechanism identified.

rows.label: TC: Strong financial profile?
rows.summary: EPS path SEK 1.62 → SEK 3.30-3.60 by FY28 (+55%, ~20% CAGR) driven by DC scaling + mix shift + cost savings.

rows.label: A1: Sufficient longevity of case (baton)?
rows.summary: DC hyperscaler buildout provides multi-year visibility (Corning $6bn Meta contract through 2030). BEAD adds N3-5Y tail.

rows.label: Q1: Three-year, mid-term, triple ratchet step-up?
rows.summary: Plausibly present. Volume (DC +37%), margin (mix shift to 15%+ DC margin), multiple (re-rate from 12.5x to 18-20x).

rows.label: A2: Sufficient growth rate?
rows.summary: Modal case ~20-25% EPS CAGR 2025-28. VF case 12-15% above SS. In GARP sweet spot.

rows.label: Q2: 12-20% EPS growth p.a.?
rows.summary: Yes — modal 20%+ EPS CAGR. Growth mix: ~60% organic DC/HE, ~25% M&A, ~15% cost savings.

rows.label: A3: Sufficient improvement in financials?
rows.summary: EBITA margin path 7.2% (2025) → 12% (2028 target). Mix shift from 15%+ DC margin vs 6.4% Fibre Solutions.

rows.label: Q3: Margin/growth step-up?
rows.summary: Clear margin step-up via mix shift. DC/HE = 40% of sales but 60% of adj. EBITA. Each % mix shift is accretive.

rows.label: TC: Acceptable case setup?
rows.summary: Demand-Driven EPSU/EPT confirmed. DC hyperscaler buildout = large exogenous demand shift. Corporate Change overlay credible.

rows.label: A4: Sufficient setup fit?
rows.summary: Demand-Driven EPSU: hyperscaler AI buildout → DC fibre demand → volume + margin expansion → EPS step-up. Classic pattern.

rows.label: Q4: Fit with required setups?
rows.summary: Strong fit. Primary: Demand-Driven EPSU/EPT. Secondary: Corporate Change (CEO + restructuring). Both legible.

rows.label: A5: Goldilocks timeliness?
rows.summary: Post-trough but not confirmed Stage 2. Entry before momentum fully confirmed — earlier than ideal but DC data supports.

rows.label: Q5: Post CfC clearing / SP turn?
rows.summary: SP turn forming post-Q4 beat but not confirmed. Base building at SEK 25-30 range.

rows.label: Q6: Less than 6M after turn?
rows.summary: Turn not yet confirmed — cannot assess timeliness relative to turn.

rows.label: TC: Sufficient case navigatability?
rows.summary: Trackable via quarterly DC organic growth, segment margins, M&A announcements. IR untested.

rows.label: A6: Sufficiently trackable, updatable, and invalidatable early?
rows.summary: Key indicators trackable: DC organic growth (quarterly), segment EBITA margins (quarterly), M&A pipeline (event-driven).

rows.label: Q7: Helpful IR re. operating momentum?
rows.summary: Not yet engaged. Unclear — need to test at ESA/DD.

rows.label: Q8: Trackable key leading indicators?
rows.summary: Yes — DC order pipeline, hyperscaler capex announcements, BEAD state allocation data, Corning/Prysmian commentary.

rows.label: TC: Earnings upgrades?
rows.summary: Post-Q4 beat triggered DNB Carnegie upgrade. VF modal 12-15% above SS. Upgrade path probable if DC sustains.

rows.label: A7: Sufficient earnings upgrades / beats?
rows.summary: Q4-25 beat: EBITA +10.8% vs consensus, revenue +8.6%. Pattern of conservative guidance reset suggests ongoing beat potential.

rows.label: Q9: Modal case 18M EPS > guidance > SS?
rows.summary: Probable. VF modal EPS 12-15% above SS consensus. If DC organic >25% in Q1-Q2 2026, 60-70% probability of positive revision.

rows.label: Q10: 3:1 SS/G EPS raise/lower skew NFY?
rows.summary: Insufficient data — only 4 covering analysts. Post-Q4 beat, 1 upgrade (DNB Carnegie). Skew turning positive but not 3:1 confirmed.

rows.label: TC: Limited drawdown risk?
rows.summary: Drawdown risk material. Bear case SEK 12-15 (40-50% downside). DC boom-bust parallel is the key risk.

rows.label: A8: Sufficiently robust / bankable multiple?
rows.summary: Blended multiple hostage to mix shift pace. DC services 15-18x vs FTTH legacy 10-12x. Partly company, partly industry driven.

rows.label: Q11: Multiple more company than exogenously driven?
rows.summary: Mixed — ~50/50 split. DC services multiple is company-driven (pivot); FTTH portion exogenously gated (rates, BEAD).

rows.label: A9: Sufficient returns?
rows.summary: Modal case: 60-100% upside (SEK 55-70). Bear case: 40-50% downside (SEK 12-15). R/R positive but asymmetry depends on DC moat.

rows.label: Q12: More than 20% 3Y TSR?
rows.summary: Yes on modal case — ~35-45% TSR p.a. if DC thesis proves out. Bear case is survivable but painful.

rows.label: IC#2: Required Case INPUTS
rows.summary: External change forces strong and confirmed: hyperscaler AI capex, NATO defence, BEAD. Duration is the question.

rows.label: TC: Sufficient change forces?
rows.summary: Strong. Three independent external forces + CEO-led internal restructuring.

rows.label: Q1: External change forces / tailwinds?
rows.summary: Strong. Hyperscaler AI buildout (Corning $6bn Meta contract), NATO 2% GDP, BEAD $42.5bn. Multi-year structural.

rows.label: Q2: Internal change forces?
rows.summary: Moderate-positive. New CEO Fröberg (ex-Ahlsell, 20+ deals). SEK 120m cost programme. 3-segment reorganisation.

rows.label: TC: Sufficient base?
rows.summary: Base mixed — DC strong, FTTH weak. No fatal headwinds but FTTH overcapacity is a drag.

rows.label: Q3: Absence of external headwinds?
rows.summary: FTTH overcapacity is a material headwind. Technology-neutral BEAD rules allow LEO/FWA competition. FX headwinds (SEK strength).

rows.label: Q3b: Well-invested base?
rows.summary: DC segment well-invested (asset-light, 1% capex). FTTH requires restructuring (SEK 120m programme). Harsh Env well-positioned.

rows.label: Q4: Thesis congruency with past/present?
rows.summary: Partially congruent. DC growth confirms mix shift narrative. But FTTH boom-bust history creates pattern risk.

rows.label: Q5: Large CfC/mispricing?
rows.summary: Yes — market prices HTRO as FTTH company (12.5x) not DC-services business (15-18x). 35% discount to peers.

rows.label: Q6: Low trading multiple?
rows.summary: 12.5x forward P/E vs DC-services peers at 15-18x. Attractive if mix shift delivers.

rows.label: IC#3: Required Case SETUPS
rows.summary: Demand-Driven EPSU/EPT plausibly present. Corporate Change overlay credible. No false-friend overlap detected.

rows.label: TC: Mechanical EPS upgrades from mis-modelling
rows.summary: Not applicable at current stage — no proprietary model built.

rows.label: A1: Mis-modelled, high-quality EPS upgrader
rows.summary: Cannot assess — no proprietary model vs SS comparison available.

rows.label: Q1: Is EPS consensus >10% below our estimate?
rows.summary: VF modal sits 12-15% above SS — suggests potential, but no formal model built.

rows.label: Q2: What specific line items are mis-modelled?
rows.summary: Likely DC segment revenue growth and margin — SS appears to underweight DC scaling.

rows.label: Q3: Is the modelling gap structural or one-off?
rows.summary: Structural if DC growth sustains — SS models likely built on pre-pivot segment mix.

rows.label: TC: Idiosyncratic, exogenous inflection
rows.summary: Primary setup — Demand-Driven EPSU/EPT. Hyperscaler AI buildout is the exogenous force.

rows.label: A1: Big demand change-driven EPSU or EPT
rows.summary: Demand-Driven EPSU validated by DC organic growth +37% FY25, +62% Q4.

rows.label: Q1: Is there a market-wide demand explosion?
rows.summary: Yes — AI/hyperscaler infrastructure buildout. DCI market ~25% CAGR to $3bn by 2028.

rows.label: Q2: Does the company hold a dominant competitive position?
rows.summary: Mid-market niche, not dominant. ~1% US FTTH share. DC services market fragmented — consolidator, not leader.

rows.label: Q3: Is the business model one-to-many scalable?
rows.summary: Services model scales via local execution + bolt-on M&A. Not platform-scalable like software.

rows.label: Q4: Is the market an oligopoly (structure AND behaviour)?
rows.summary: No — fibre market is fragmented with intense competition. DC services more fragmented still. Not oligopolistic.

rows.label: Q5: Is there fixed cost leverage for margin expansion?
rows.summary: Limited — services model is variable-cost heavy. Leverage comes from mix shift to higher-margin DC, not fixed cost absorption.

rows.label: TC: Huge internal push/improvement
rows.summary: Secondary setup — Corporate Change. New CEO + cost programme + M&A engine.

rows.label: A1: Strong internal changes driven EPSU / EPT
rows.summary: CEO Fröberg driving 3-pronged restructuring: segment reorganisation, cost programme, M&A pivot to DC.

rows.label: Q1: Is management credible on execution?
rows.summary: Early signs positive — conservative guidance reset, Q3-Q4 beats. Ex-Ahlsell (20+ deals). Untested in fibre industry.

rows.label: Q2: What is the specific internal change programme?
rows.summary: Clear: SEK 120m cost savings, 3-segment reorganisation, decentralised M&A (3-6 deals/year at 5-7x EBITDA).

rows.label: Q3: What is the track record of delivery so far?
rows.summary: 12 months only. Q3-Q4 beats after H1 miss. Weterings closure is a capital destruction flag. Too early.

rows.label: TC: Clearing of (idiosyncratic) negative sentiment
rows.summary: Not primary setup — but FTTH sentiment clearing could provide secondary catalyst.

rows.label: A1: Oversold/rebounding quality compounder
rows.summary: Not applicable as primary setup. HTRO is not a quality compounder at this stage.

rows.label: Q1: Is the negative sentiment idiosyncratic or sector-wide?
rows.summary: FTTH sentiment is sector-wide. DC sentiment is positive. Mixed.

rows.label: Q2: Is the underlying business quality intact despite sentiment?
rows.summary: DC quality is intact and accelerating. FTTH quality is genuinely impaired.

rows.label: Q3: What specific catalyst clears the overhang?
rows.summary: BEAD materialisation + DC mix shift diluting FTTH impact on group numbers.

rows.label: A2: Huge C/C clearing event in solid quality (CVS-type)
rows.summary: Not applicable.

rows.label: Q1: Is there a specific clearing event identified?
rows.summary: Not applicable.

rows.label: Q2: Is business quality sufficient to justify rebound post-clearing?
rows.summary: Not applicable.

rows.label: TC: Standard exogenous reversion to mean
rows.summary: Not applicable — HTRO is not a mean-reversion play.

rows.label: A1: Mean-reverting quality business post-temporary headwind
rows.summary: Not applicable — FTTH headwind may be structural, not temporary.

rows.label: Q1: Is the headwind genuinely temporary?
rows.summary: FTTH headwind may be structural (LEO satellite competition, overcapacity). Not confirmed temporary.

rows.label: Q2: What is the normalised earnings level to revert to?
rows.summary: Not applicable.

rows.label: Q3: Is the quality of the franchise intact through the headwind?
rows.summary: DC franchise intact and growing. FTTH franchise quality impaired.

rows.label: TC: Trough-on-trough turn in solid quality cyclical
rows.summary: Partially applicable — FTTH segment is at/near trough. But HTRO is not a pure cyclical play.

rows.label: A1: Cyclical quality compounder at trough earnings
rows.summary: FTTH segment at cyclical trough (6.4% EBITA margin vs 15% target). Group earnings depressed by FTTH drag.

rows.label: Q1: Are earnings genuinely at trough?
rows.summary: FTTH segment likely at/near trough. Group earnings suppressed by mix. DC is accelerating, not at trough.

rows.label: Q2: Is business quality sufficient to hold through the full cycle?
rows.summary: DC segment quality supports holding. FTTH quality is questionable — structural decline possible.

rows.label: Q3: What leading indicators confirm the cyclical turn?
rows.summary: BEAD state allocations, Corning FTTH commentary, housing starts. Early positive signals but not confirmed.

rows.label: P4: Fundamental Case (Inputs)
rows.summary: Foundation quality mixed — strong DC economics, weak FTTH. Moderate complexity (3 segments, 9 countries). M&A execution ORANGE.

rows.label: BB#1: Required Simplicity Guardrails
rows.summary: 4 KDs narrowly focused. But 18 production sites, 9 countries, 3 segments = moderate complexity. Customer concentration flag.

rows.label: TC: Narrowly focused bet?
rows.summary: 2 fulcrum + 3 key drivers = manageable. Focus is narrow.

rows.label: Q1: 2 or less fulcrum drivers and 4 or less key drivers?
rows.summary: Yes — 2 fulcrum (DC moat, M&A execution) + 3 key drivers (FTTH recovery, CEO quality, EPS bridge). Narrowly focused.

rows.label: TC: Narrow perimeter?
rows.summary: 3 segments × 9 countries = 27 units. Above ideal but manageable as DC/HE are the focus segments.

rows.label: Q2: 10 or less geographies x business units?
rows.summary: No — 3 segments × 9 countries = 27. Above threshold. Mitigant: DC + HE = 2 focus segments.

rows.label: TC: No headwinds?
rows.summary: FTTH overcapacity and FX headwinds present. DC has no material headwinds.

rows.label: Q3: Zero value chain headwinds to revenue?
rows.summary: No — FTTH overcapacity, LEO satellite competition, FX (SEK strength -9% impact). DC clean.

rows.label: TC: Narrow range of issues?
rows.summary: Two primary uncertainty axes: DC moat durability and M&A execution quality.

rows.label: Q4: 2 or less CfCs or problems?
rows.summary: Borderline — 2 fulcrum concerns (DC moat, M&A) + 1 structural issue (FTTH overcapacity) = 3. Slightly above ideal.

rows.label: TC: Conservative IR?
rows.summary: New CEO appears conservative (reset expectations, then beat Q3-Q4). But only 12 months of data.

rows.label: Q5: Company confirms conservative guidance?
rows.summary: Pattern suggests conservative-setter behaviour. H1-25 miss followed by Q3-Q4 beats. Need more data points.

rows.label: TC: Clear investment case?
rows.summary: Clear: DC demand → volume + margin mix shift → EPS step-up. Transmission mechanism legible.

rows.label: Q6: Clear strategy-to-EPS transmission mechanism?
rows.summary: Yes — DC scaling + mix shift + cost savings → EBITA margin recovery → EPS growth. Quantifiable.

rows.label: TC: Trackable case drivers?
rows.summary: DC organic growth (quarterly), segment margins, M&A activity are trackable. BEAD less so.

rows.label: Q7: Clear VC/Co inputs-to-EPS transmission mechanism?
rows.summary: Partially — DC demand trackable via hyperscaler capex announcements. FTTH recovery harder to track (BEAD timing uncertain).

rows.label: BB#2: Required Foundation Quality
rows.summary: ESA confirms: DC services moat narrow but defensible via local execution + installed base stickiness. FTTH remains commoditised.

rows.label: TC: Supportive Industry Structure
rows.summary: Fibre industry fragmented with intense competition from vertical integrators. DC services market more favourable.

rows.label: A: Concentrated existing market structure
rows.summary: FTTH market dominated by Corning/Prysmian. HTRO has ~1% US share. Not concentrated in HTRO's favour.

rows.label: Q: This company #1 (or #2)?
rows.summary: No — HTRO is a small player in global fibre. Mid-market niche in DC services.

rows.label: Q: Top 5 > 30% share?
rows.summary: In DC services mid-market, possibly. In overall fibre, no.

rows.label: Q: Steep market share distribution curve?
rows.summary: No — fragmented market. Corning/Prysmian are giants but market is not concentrated.

rows.label: A: Concentrating gravitational pull
rows.summary: DC services market fragmenting → consolidation opportunity. FTTH market stable/declining.

rows.label: Q: Stability of share over time?
rows.summary: HTRO's share has been volatile — grew via M&A 2021-23, then contracted with FTTH downturn.

rows.label: Q: New entrants' share?
rows.summary: DC services seeing new entrants as hyperscaler demand grows. FTTH barriers low.

rows.label: Q: Share consolidates towards largest competitors over time?
rows.summary: Yes for FTTH (Corning/Prysmian gaining). DC services still fragmenting.

rows.label: Q: Share consolidates to most advantaged competitors?
rows.summary: In FTTH, yes — vertical integrators winning. In DC services, local service capability matters more.

rows.label: A: Benign competitor attitudes + stability thereof
rows.summary: Competitive attitudes moderately benign — Corning/Prysmian focused on Tier-1. Could pivot if Tier-1 slows.

rows.label: Q: Benign-ness?
rows.summary: Currently benign — giants focused on hyperscaler direct supply. Risk: pivot to mid-market if Tier-1 slows.

rows.label: Q: MOL instability or stable attitude?
rows.summary: Potentially unstable — if hyperscaler capex pauses, Corning/Prysmian may redirect capacity.

rows.label: Q: Shark attack if weakness sensed?
rows.summary: Scale advantage overwhelming in a price war. Corning/Prysmian could undercut HTRO in FTTH.

rows.label: Q: Volume / ROCE prioritisation?
rows.summary: FTTH shows volume prioritisation over ROCE (overcapacity dynamics). DC services more ROCE-focused.

rows.label: A: Competitiveness trends
rows.summary: DC services competitiveness improving via M&A consolidation. FTTH competitiveness deteriorating.

rows.label: TC: Supportive Value Chain Dynamics
rows.summary: Unfavourable — no vertical integration into glass preform (70% of industry profit pool).

rows.label: A: Demand side
rows.summary: DC demand structural and growing. FTTH demand cyclical and uncertain.

rows.label: Q: Customer demand?
rows.summary: DC: strong, hyperscaler-driven, multi-year. FTTH: weak, BEAD-dependent. HE: steady, defence-driven.

rows.label: Q: Route to market / intermediaries?
rows.summary: Direct to hyperscalers/enterprises for DC. Through contractors for FTTH. No disintermediation risk.

rows.label: A: Competition
rows.summary: Intense from vertical integrators in FTTH. Fragmented in DC services — consolidation opportunity.

rows.label: Q: Existing competition?
rows.summary: Corning, Prysmian, Nexans in FTTH. DC services fragmented — smaller local players.

rows.label: Q: Adjacencies entering?
rows.summary: Risk: Corning/Prysmian pivoting to DC services. Opportunity: IT integrators lack fibre expertise.

rows.label: Q: Vanilla new entrants?
rows.summary: Low barrier in FTTH distribution. Higher in DC services (requires local expertise + certifications).

rows.label: Q: Orthogonal / innovative new entrants?
rows.summary: LEO satellite (Starlink) is an orthogonal entrant to FTTH. No equivalent in DC services.

rows.label: Q: Substitutes / alternatives?
rows.summary: LEO/FWA for FTTH in rural markets. No viable substitute for fibre in DC interconnect.

rows.label: A: Disruption forces
rows.summary: LEO satellite is a medium-term disruption risk for FTTH TAM. AI workloads create disruption opportunity for DC.

rows.label: A: Political / regulatory
rows.summary: BEAD is supportive (fibre subsidies). NATO spending supportive for HE. No adverse regulatory risk identified.

rows.label: Q: Regulators?
rows.summary: No adverse regulatory risk. Fibre infrastructure is politically supported across EU and US.

rows.label: Q: Political actors?
rows.summary: BEAD, NATO, EU digital infrastructure investment all supportive. Technology-neutral rules are minor risk.

rows.label: A: Supplier side
rows.summary: Not vertically integrated into glass preform. Dependent on third-party glass producers. Supply shortage risk.

rows.label: TC: Advantaged Business / Widening Moats (SRCA)
rows.summary: DC services moat narrow: local execution + installed base stickiness, not IP or scale. FTTH moat absent.

rows.label: A: Strong operational/competitive advantages (MOAT)
rows.summary: Narrow and segment-specific. DC: local execution, day-two service contracts. FTTH: no moat.

rows.label: Q: Customer-related moats?
rows.summary: DC: installed base stickiness + day-two service contracts. FTTH: low switching costs.

rows.label: Q: Production-related moats?
rows.summary: Limited — manufacturing is not highly differentiated. Microduct technology is not proprietary.

rows.label: Q: Scale advantage moats?
rows.summary: No scale advantage — ~1% market share. Corning/Prysmian have overwhelming scale in FTTH.

rows.label: Q: Regulatory/political moats?
rows.summary: None identified. Fibre market is open.

rows.label: Q: Culture/knowledge moats?
rows.summary: Local service expertise in DC installation is a knowledge moat. Decentralised model preserves entrepreneurial culture.

rows.label: Q: High relative scale (to competitors)?
rows.summary: No — small relative to Corning/Prysmian. Mid-market player in DC services.

rows.label: Q: Large realistic TAM?
rows.summary: DC services TAM growing at ~25% CAGR to $3bn by 2028. Large enough for HTRO's growth targets.

rows.label: A: Strong operational outputs (OPERATE)
rows.summary: DC segment operational outputs strong: 15.3% EBITA margin, asset-light, 235% cash conversion Q4-25.

rows.label: Q: High SCU?
rows.summary: Mixed — DC high, FTTH low. Blended group SCU below peers.

rows.label: Q: Leading or non-inferior RSCU?
rows.summary: DC services competitive. FTTH inferior to vertically integrated peers.

rows.label: Q: High, utilised pricing power?
rows.summary: Limited pricing power in FTTH (commodity). DC services have moderate pricing power (service differentiation).

rows.label: Q: Scale economies shared?
rows.summary: Not applicable at current scale. Would emerge if M&A consolidation succeeds.

rows.label: A: Strong financial outputs (GENERATE)
rows.summary: DC financial outputs strong. Group-level suppressed by FTTH drag.

rows.label: Q: 12-20% EPS growth?
rows.summary: Modal case achieves 20%+ CAGR. But from a depressed base — not proven sustainable.

rows.label: Q: Scope to deploy at high RONIC?
rows.summary: M&A pipeline at 5-7x EBITDA implies strong RONIC on acquisitions. Organic DC growth is capital-light.

rows.label: Q: High post-tax RONIC (25-30%+)?
rows.summary: DC segment likely yes. Group blended RONIC depressed by FTTH. Need financial model.

rows.label: Q: High NI to FCF conversion (80%)?
rows.summary: Yes — Q4-25 cash conversion 235%. Low capex (1% of sales). Strong WC management.

rows.label: Q: Post-tax ROCE of 15%+?
rows.summary: Not at current margins. Would achieve at 12% EBITA target. Goodwill-heavy balance sheet depresses ROCE.

rows.label: Q: Post-tax ROTC of 30%+?
rows.summary: Unlikely given current margin profile and goodwill base.

rows.label: Q: High gross margins?
rows.summary: 38.5% gross margin (2025). Below peers (Prysmian 46%, Nexans 42%). Compression from 42% peak.

rows.label: Q: Solid EBIT margins?
rows.summary: 7.6% EBIT margin (2025). Below 20% threshold. Target 12% EBITA by 2028. Work to do.

rows.label: Q: Genuine fixed cost operating leverage?
rows.summary: Limited in services model. Leverage comes from mix shift, not fixed cost absorption.

rows.label: Q: Low tax rate?
rows.summary: Swedish corporate tax 20.6%. Reasonable. No unusual tax leakage identified.

rows.label: A: Gets stronger as it gets bigger (Gravitational pull)
rows.summary: M&A consolidation in DC services could create scale advantages over time. Not evident yet.

rows.label: Q: Mechanical forces drive SRCA improvement vs peers?
rows.summary: Potential via M&A consolidation — larger installed base creates service stickiness. Not proven.

rows.label: Q: Mechanical forces drive improving financial outputs vs peers?
rows.summary: Mix shift mechanically improves group financials as DC grows faster than FTTH.

rows.label: A: Plausible scope for mid-term improvement (SCOPE)
rows.summary: Clear scope: DC scaling + FTTH cost programme + M&A. Three independent improvement paths.

rows.label: Q: Peer precedents?
rows.summary: No direct peer precedent for FTTH-to-DC services pivot at this scale.

rows.label: Q: Big savings/improvements in a few areas?
rows.summary: SEK 120m cost programme in Fibre Solutions. DC margin expansion via mix shift.

rows.label: Q: Lots of little savings in many areas?
rows.summary: Decentralised M&A model creates many small improvement opportunities. Not yet demonstrated.

rows.label: TC: Great Operator / Allocator
rows.summary: New CEO — early signs positive but untested in fibre industry stress. M&A background relevant.

rows.label: A: Right direction
rows.summary: Strategic pivot to DC/HE is directionally correct. Cost programme in Fibre Solutions appropriate.

rows.label: Q: Big ambition/vision?
rows.summary: SEK 11bn revenue target by 2028 with 12% EBITA margin. Ambitious relative to current SEK 7.5bn / 7.2%.

rows.label: Q: Great, smart strategy?
rows.summary: DC pivot is smart. Question is execution capability, not strategic direction.

rows.label: A: Right resources
rows.summary: CEO 12 months in. Board + mgmt own ~13%. Team untested in new configuration.

rows.label: Q: Great CEO?
rows.summary: Fröberg has relevant background (Ahlsell, 20+ deals) but untested in fibre industry.

rows.label: Q: Built great leadership team?
rows.summary: Too early — Martin Åberg (DC) is key. Team stability post-reorganisation TBD.

rows.label: Q: Well-resourced/well-invested?
rows.summary: DC segment well-invested (Communication Zone acquisition). Fibre Solutions needs restructuring.

rows.label: Q: Holistic culture + alignment?
rows.summary: Decentralised model — culture alignment across 9 countries untested under Fröberg.

rows.label: Q: Conservative guidance?
rows.summary: Pattern suggests conservative-setter — Q3-Q4 beats after H1 miss reset. Need more data.

rows.label: Q: Clear communication?
rows.summary: Improving — 3-segment reporting adds clarity. Still limited IR engagement depth.

rows.label: A: Right record / action / behaviour
rows.summary: 12 months of data only. One capital destruction event (Weterings). Q3-Q4 beats are positive.

rows.label: Q: High RONIC capital allocation?
rows.summary: JOWO acquisition at 4.7x EBITDA is good. Weterings closure is a flag. Mixed evidence.

rows.label: Q: Acts not just talks?
rows.summary: Cost programme executing. Segment reorganisation done. M&A activity restarting.

rows.label: Q: Track record of delivery?
rows.summary: Too early — 12 months. Q3-Q4 beats are positive data points.

rows.label: Q: Outlier standards?
rows.summary: Not yet demonstrated. Need more data.

rows.label: Q: Shareholders first?
rows.summary: Zero dividend policy — reinvesting. Board/mgmt own ~13%. Aligned but not aggressive.

rows.label: Q: Dynamic repurchaser?
rows.summary: No buyback programme. Zero dividend. All capital allocated to M&A + organic growth.

rows.label: Q: Prudent governance?
rows.summary: No red flags. Erik Selin related-party lease is immaterial (SEK 7m/year). Standard governance.

rows.label: BB#3: Fit for Market Paradigm
rows.summary: AI/DC infrastructure paradigm-aligned. Defence fits risk-off. Exit discipline via 10 ACHs.

rows.label: TC: Exit discipline when negatives manifest?
rows.summary: 10 HTRO-specific ACH invalidation scenarios armed. Exit triggers clearly defined.

rows.label: Q1: Market cycle — downturn?
rows.summary: DC demand structural, not cyclical. But hyperscaler capex could exhibit cycle. Exit: DC organic <+10% for 2 consecutive Q.

rows.label: Q2: AI disruption?
rows.summary: HTRO benefits from AI — DC fibre is the physical layer. No disruption risk from AI; it's the enabler.

rows.label: Q3: Input cost inflation (Iran)?
rows.summary: No Iran/Middle East exposure. HE segment benefits from defence escalation. Tariff risk limited (<5%).

rows.label: Q4: No overlap with 10x general invalidating ACHs?
rows.summary: No overlap identified at current assessment. 10 HTRO-specific ACHs armed.

rows.label: BB#4: Strenuously Seek-to-Avoid Constraints
rows.summary: YELLOW flag. FTTH boom-bust risk. Bear case SEK 12-15 (40-50% downside). Multiple partly industry-driven.

rows.label: TC: No fundamental SHMLP risks?
rows.summary: DC boom-bust is a material risk. Bear case downside 40-50%.

rows.label: Q: What is downside to trough SP?
rows.summary: Redeye bear case SEK 12-15. 40-50% downside from current ~SEK 28.

rows.label: Q: Can thesis survive a -30% drawdown?
rows.summary: At starter size (1.0-1.5%), a -30% drawdown is manageable. Would require ACH re-assessment.

rows.label: Q: What invalidates at trough?
rows.summary: DC boom-bust confirmation + FTTH permanent impairment. 10 ACH scenarios cover this.

rows.label: TC: No wide skew in outcomes?
rows.summary: Wide skew: bull SEK 55-70 vs bear SEK 12-15. 4-5x range.

rows.label: Q: Range of bull vs bear case EPS >50%?
rows.summary: Yes — very wide. Bull 2028 EPS SEK 3.30-3.60 vs bear SEK 0.80-1.00. >200% range.

rows.label: Q: Is case predictability high or low?
rows.summary: Low at group level — DC accelerating but FTTH uncertain. Two segments pulling opposite directions.

rows.label: Q: How many independent variables?
rows.summary: 5+ major variables: DC growth rate, M&A execution, FTTH recovery, BEAD timing, competitive dynamics.

rows.label: TC: No large downside to trough multiple?
rows.summary: FTTH peers have traded at 6-8x trough EBITDA. Current 12.5x P/E has downside to 8-10x.

rows.label: Q: What is trough EV/EBIT for this sector?
rows.summary: Fibre peers: 8-10x trough EV/EBITDA. DC services peers haven't been through a trough.

rows.label: Q: How far is current multiple from trough?
rows.summary: Current ~12.5x P/E. Trough could be 8-10x. 20-35% multiple downside risk.

rows.label: Q: What drove the last trough?
rows.summary: FTTH overcapacity + interest rate impact on housing. 2023-24 trough well-documented.

rows.label: TC: Multiple more company than industry?
rows.summary: ~50/50 company vs industry. DC mix shift is company-driven; FTTH portion industry-driven.

rows.label: Q: Correlation of stock multiple to sector multiple?
rows.summary: High correlation to Nordic mid-cap industrials and fibre peers.

rows.label: Q: What % of SP move is company vs. sector driven?
rows.summary: Estimated 50/50. DC pivot creates company-specific catalyst; FTTH ties to sector.

rows.label: Q: Does valuation hold if sector de-rates?
rows.summary: Partially — DC business should hold but FTTH would de-rate with sector.

rows.label: BB#5: Small Size Constraints
rows.summary: Market cap SEK 5.8bn (~EUR 0.5bn). Below ideal but above minimum. Liquidity adequate.

rows.label: TC: Sufficient market cap?
rows.summary: SEK 5.8bn = ~EUR 0.5bn. Below sweet spot ($5-50bn) but not disqualifying.

rows.label: Q: $10m ADV minimum met?
rows.summary: Yes — 1.7m shares/day × SEK 28 = ~SEK 48m/day (~$4.6m). Close to threshold.

rows.label: Q: $1.5bn market cap minimum met?
rows.summary: No — SEK 5.8bn = ~$560m. Below $1.5bn minimum. Size constraint flag.

rows.label: Q: Can position be built without moving the price?
rows.summary: At 1.0-1.5% portfolio, yes. At target size (3%), may need multi-day accumulation.

rows.label: TC: Sufficient liquidity?
rows.summary: Adequate for starter position. Constrained for larger sizing.

rows.label: Q: Bid-ask spread acceptable (<50bps)?
rows.summary: Nasdaq Stockholm — spread typically <50bps for mid-cap names.

rows.label: Q: Average daily volume supports 5-day entry/exit?
rows.summary: 1.7m shares/day. At 1.5% portfolio, ~5-day entry feasible.

rows.label: Q: Float sufficient (>50% free float)?
rows.summary: No controlling shareholder. Handelsbanken Fonder 9.3% is largest. Estimated ~70% free float.

rows.label: BB#6: Invalidating Constraints
rows.summary: No immediate invalidation flags. DC performance non-mediocre. FTTH mediocre but being restructured.

rows.label: TC: No slowing of core engine?
rows.summary: DC engine accelerating (+62% Q4 organic). Fibre Solutions engine declining but being restructured.

rows.label: Q: Is core business momentum intact?
rows.summary: DC momentum strong. Group momentum mixed due to FTTH drag.

rows.label: Q: Any degradation in unit economics?
rows.summary: FTTH unit economics deteriorating (margin compression). DC unit economics improving.

rows.label: TC: No mediocre CEO/weird choices?
rows.summary: No weird choices. Weterings closure is a flag but explicable. Strategic direction coherent.

rows.label: Q: Any questionable strategic decisions?
rows.summary: Weterings closure (acquired 2021 for EUR 5.6m, closed 2025). Capital destruction but small.

rows.label: Q: CEO track record consistent?
rows.summary: 12 months only. Conservative reset + Q3-Q4 beats. Consistent so far but insufficient data.

rows.label: TC: No big 'Hmmms'?
rows.summary: DC boom-bust question is the 'Hmmm'. Not a red flag, but requires monitoring.

rows.label: Q: Any gut-level discomfort?
rows.summary: DC boom-bust parallel with FTTH history creates legitimate discomfort.

rows.label: Q: Anything that doesn't add up?
rows.summary: Non-GAAP adjustments increasing (SEK 202m Q3, SEK 97m Q4). Explicable but worth monitoring.

rows.label: TC: No red flags (2+ Fs)?
rows.summary: 1 F (relative scale). No cluster of F ratings.

rows.label: Q: Count of F-rated attributes?
rows.summary: Zero F-rated at pillar/family level. Some D/F at individual question level (scale, market share).

rows.label: Q: Are any F-ratings in critical areas?
rows.summary: No F-ratings in critical areas. D on relative scale is structural, not thesis-breaking.

rows.label: TC: No mediocrity (8+ Ds)?
rows.summary: Several C ratings but limited D cluster. Not mediocre — DC segment distinctly non-mediocre.

rows.label: Q: Count of D-rated attributes?
rows.summary: ~4-5 D ratings (scale, market share, concentration). Below 8 threshold.

rows.label: Q: Pattern of weakness or isolated?
rows.summary: Isolated — concentrated in structural scale disadvantage, not operational weakness.

rows.label: TC: No recent earnings cuts?
rows.summary: Post-Q4 beat, revisions turning positive. No recent cuts.

rows.label: Q: Any SS estimate cuts in L3M?
rows.summary: No — post-Q4 beat triggered upgrade (DNB Carnegie). Revisions positive.

rows.label: Q: Direction of consensus revisions?
rows.summary: Turning positive. 2 Buy / 1 Hold / 1 Sell. DNB Carnegie TP upgrade SEK 38→42.50.

rows.label: TC: No peer problems?
rows.summary: Corning seeing FTTH inflection. Prysmian strong. No peer profit warnings in DC services.

rows.label: Q: Are sector peers showing deterioration?
rows.summary: No — Corning seeing FTTH inflection, Prysmian reporting strong cable demand.

rows.label: Q: Any peer profit warnings in L6M?
rows.summary: No peer profit warnings in DC services or cable sector.

rows.label: TC: No negative lessons overlap?
rows.summary: FTTH boom-bust pattern overlaps with known cyclical traps. DC services is new — no historical parallel.

rows.label: Q: Overlap with past losing patterns?
rows.summary: FTTH cycle mirrors classic boom-bust cyclical trap. DC services is genuinely different (services vs product).

rows.label: Q: Similarity to previously failed investments?
rows.summary: No direct parallel. Check Fasadgruppen/XVIVO for Nordic mid-cap liquidity lessons.

rows.label: TC: No false friend setup?
rows.summary: No false-friend overlap detected. Demand-Driven EPSU/EPT is a valid setup classification.

rows.label: Q: Does case fit any unacceptable setup profile?
rows.summary: No — Demand-Driven EPSU/EPT is an approved setup type.

rows.label: Q: Is apparent quality actually a complexity trap?
rows.summary: Risk — 3 segments, 9 countries, M&A-dependent growth could be a complexity trap. Monitor.

rows.label: BB#7: Past Trend / Momentum Constraints
rows.summary: Improving — post-trough, MAs turning up. SS revisions positive. Peers inflecting.

rows.label: BB#8: Nice-to-Have Attributes
rows.summary: No dividend. No buyback. BEAD acceleration and edge DC proliferation are positive optionality.

rows.label: TC: Additional upside?
rows.summary: BEAD acceleration, edge DC proliferation, defence budget escalation. Multiple re-rating on mix shift.

rows.label: Q: Cash return yield >3%?
rows.summary: No — zero dividend policy. All cash reinvested.

rows.label: Q: Progressive dividend policy?
rows.summary: No dividend policy. Reinvesting in growth + M&A.

rows.label: Q: Share buyback programme active?
rows.summary: No buyback programme.

rows.label: TC: Self-righting behaviour ATM?
rows.summary: Cost programme demonstrates self-righting. Cash conversion exceptional (235% Q4-25).

rows.label: Q: Management has significant skin in game (>5%)?
rows.summary: Board + mgmt own ~13%. Above 5% threshold. Aligned.

rows.label: Q: Company featured in quality screens?
rows.summary: Not currently — margins too low. Would appear if EBITA reaches 12% target.

rows.label: Q: Institutional ownership increasing?
rows.summary: Stable — Handelsbanken Fonder 9.3%, Accendo Capital 5.9%. No major inflows detected.

rows.label: P5: Sell-Side Momentum
rows.summary: 2 Buy / 1 Hold / 1 Sell. Revisions turning positive post-Q4 beat. Limited coverage depth.

rows.label: EPS revisions
rows.summary: L1M +2-3% · L3M +5-7% · L6M flat (limited data)

rows.summary: Post-Q4 beat, modest positive revisions.

rows.label: L3M
rows.summary: Turning positive — recovery from H1-25 miss.

rows.label: L6M
rows.summary: Flat to slightly negative — H1-25 miss still in window.

rows.label: EBITDA revisions
rows.summary: L1M positive · L3M positive · L6M mixed

rows.label: L1M
rows.summary: Post-Q4 EBITA beat +10.8% driving positive revisions.

rows.label: L3M
rows.summary: Positive trend from Q3-Q4 beats.

rows.label: L6M
rows.summary: Mixed — H1-25 negative, H2-25 positive.

rows.label: Revenue revisions
rows.summary: L1M flat · L3M slightly positive · L6M negative

rows.label: L1M
rows.summary: Revenue revisions muted — DC strong but FTTH offsetting.

rows.label: L3M
rows.summary: Slightly positive — DC contribution lifting group.

rows.label: L6M
rows.summary: Negative — FTTH weakness dominated L6M window.

rows.label: Price target revisions
rows.summary: L1M DNB Carnegie upgrade SEK 38→42.50 · L3M positive

rows.label: L1M
rows.summary: DNB Carnegie TP upgrade SEK 38→42.50 post-Q4 beat.

rows.label: L3M
rows.summary: Positive — 1 upgrade, 0 downgrades in L3M.

rows.label: L6M
rows.summary: Mixed — some downgrades during H1-25 weakness.

rows.label: % Buy change
rows.summary: Stable — 2 Buy / 1 Hold / 1 Sell. Limited coverage.

rows.summary: No rating changes in L1M.

rows.label: L3M
rows.summary: Stable — limited coverage makes % changes noisy.

rows.label: L6M
rows.summary: Stable — Kepler Hold and Danske Sell are potential converts if Q1-26 DC >30%.

rows.label: P6: Valuation
rows.summary: 12.5x forward P/E vs DC-services peers 15-18x. 35% discount. If mix shift delivers, re-rate to 18-20x.

rows.label: 2YF P/E — absolute
rows.summary: ~12.5x on FY26E. Attractive for mix-shift story.

rows.label: 2YF P/E vs own history
rows.summary: ~30th percentile of 5-year range. Near historical lows (reflecting FTTH trough).

rows.label: 2YF P/E vs peers
rows.summary: 35% discount to DC-services peer group (15-18x). Discount reflects FTTH drag + size.

blocks.text: _Post-trough recovery underway; MAs turning up but Stage 2 not confirmed. Technical setup is forming, not mature._
SP ~SEK 28, well below SEK 65+ peak. Post-trough base forming on Q4-25 beat volume.
Estimated 4-5 / 9 PASS. Key fails: 200D MA trend not confirmed, 150D/200D golden cross missing, RS Rank likely below 70.

items.text: **PASS (5):** Price > 200D MA, Price > 150D MA, 50D > 150D MA, Price > 50D MA, Price > 30% above 52W low, Price within 25% of 52W high.
**FAIL (3-4):** 200D MA not yet trending up (flattening only), 150D not above 200D (golden cross pending), RS Rank likely below 70.
**Need FactSet confirmation** for precise MA levels and RS Rank.

blocks.text: _Technical picture is constructive but not confirmed. Stage 1→2 transition is the key technical catalyst. Monitor MAs for golden cross confirmation._
_DC segment is the thesis. Mix shift from FTTH (6.4% margin) to DC (15.3% margin) is the EPS engine. Change forces confirmed; moat question outstanding._
Change forces confirmed and large. DC organic +37% FY25, +62% Q4. Dual setup: Demand-Driven EPSU/EPT (primary) + Corporate Change (secondary). Conviction C+ upper.

EPS path plausibly present. Triple ratchet: volume (DC scaling +62% Q4 organic) + margin (mix shift from 5% FS to 15% DC) + multiple (re-rate from 12.5x to 18-20x). Modal case: FY28 EPS SEK 3.30-3.60 vs FY25 ~SEK 1.62. Conviction C+ upper.

items.text: **IC#1 Required Case OUTPUTS:** Triple ratchet path is plausible but back-loaded. DC organic growth trajectory (39% Q3, 62% Q4 2025) combined with HE +11% and FS cost programme creates credible path to 20%+ EPS CAGR 2025-28. Key constraint: M&A execution in DC must deliver without margin dilution.

**IC#1 RA1 — Sufficient longevity of case (baton)?** Yes, conditionally. DC megatrend (hyperscaler capex, AI infrastructure) has multi-year runway. Management 2028 targets imply 3Y+ execution horizon. Risk: Fibre Solutions drag could shorten the window if it absorbs management attention.

**IC#1 CQ1 — Three-year triple ratchet step-up:** Plausibly present. DC organic growth trajectory (39% Q3, 62% Q4 2025) combined with HE +11% and Fibre Solutions cost programme creates a credible path to 20%+ EPS CAGR over 2025-28.

sub.text: VF modal case: FY28 EPS SEK 3.30-3.60 vs FY25 ~SEK 1.62. Triple ratchet requires sustained DC scaling + mix shift + cost savings.
Management 2028 targets: SEK 11bn revenue, 12% EBITA margin. SS consensus lower at SEK 9.3bn / 9.9% margin — gap is M&A-dependent.

items.text: **IC#1 RA2 — Sufficient growth rate?** Yes on modal case. SS consensus implies ~20-25% EPS CAGR 2025-28. VF case sits 12-15% above SS. Comfortably in GARP sweet spot if DC growth sustains above 30%.
